primary_observation: M1 preserved the original source-zero collapse. Stage 3 only labelled one sink and one off-target taint propagator, while Stage 4 still exposed CronParser/CronParserField/FieldParser parameter sources but not the true entry CronValidator.isValid(String value)#value; final alarms and paths stayed at zero.

M2 changed the structured-label failure shape after injecting a minimal source-window into Stage 3/4 prompts, but it still did not retain Throwable.getMessage() or buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(String p0); final alarms and paths stayed at zero.

M3 restored target-aligned reasoning: the constrained JSON output explicitly named CronValidator.isValid(String value) as the true entry, Throwable.getMessage() as the message bridge, and buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(String p0) as the sink.
M4 preserved the same target-aligned chain in freeform text, showing that once the model was explicitly anchored to the target chain, removing the JSON schema did not degrade the explanation.

M5's constrained retrieval loop requested no extra files and still produced the same target-aligned audit note, implying that after M3/M4 the starting bundle was already sufficient and extra repo traversal was unnecessary.
